properties.Test Case: 2) In YouTrack, respond using @reporter. Verify the reporter's email receives a response.
properties.Steps: 2) In YouTrack, respond using @reporter. Verify the reporter's email receives a response.

properties.Test Case: 3) In reporter's email, send a response back to the Bloom Team. Verify the response shows up in Youtrack. Note: in one test, the response took a good 10 minutes.
properties.Steps: 3) In reporter's email, send a response back to the Bloom Team. Verify the response shows up in Youtrack. Note: in one test, the response took a good 10 minutes.

properties.Test Case: Report a problem then click on "Learn More". Ensure there is no "close" button.
properties.Steps: Report a problem then click on "Learn More". Ensure there is no "close" button.

properties.Test Case: With a fresh machine (VM or uninstall Bloom and erase all vestiges of Bloom: go to C:/Users/<name>/AppData/Local/SIL/Bloom. Remove all directories that start with 5.X. There may be other directories as well in that area that aren't so obvious named something like ~/AppData/Local/SIL/Bloom.exe_Url_<guid?>.): Register for an earlier version of Bloom, then upgrade Bloom. Ensure your registration info has been saved.
properties.Steps: With a fresh machine (VM or uninstall Bloom and erase all vestiges of Bloom: go to C:/Users/<name>/AppData/Local/SIL/Bloom.  Remove all directories that start with 5.X.  There may be other directories as well in that area that aren't so obvious named something like ~/AppData/Local/SIL/Bloom.exe_Url_<guid?>.):  Register for an earlier version of Bloom, then upgrade Bloom. Ensure your registration info has been saved.

properties.Test Case: Edit collection file to intentionally corrupt its integrity. Try booting Bloom and also try navigating to a corrupt collection. Ensure error handling works.
properties.Steps: Edit collection file to intentionally corrupt its integrity. Try booting Bloom and also try navigating to a corrupt collection. Ensure error handling works.
